mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward returns for the S&P 500 over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that sustains corporate earnings growth and drives positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce a statistically significant positive return differential (p > 0.05) over 60-day forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during periods of low inflation reflects healthy capacity utilization without pricing pressure, which supports equity valuations and yields positive monthly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth coincides with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in over 55% of low-inflation observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls directly correlate with expanding economic activity and corporate revenue expectations, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS month-over-month growth shows a negative or zero Rank IC against 5-day forward returns across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A widening spread between real GDP growth and the federal funds rate indicates accommodative monetary policy relative to economic output, which fuels asset price appreciation over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-to-FEDFUNDS ratio fails to predict quarterly return direction with an accuracy exceeding 52% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in real personal consumption expenditures exceeding 0.5% signals a sudden consumer pullback that immediately depresses equity valuations and produces negative monthly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where RSAFS drops below the 0.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects capacity constraints and rising input costs that compress profit margins, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth during tightening periods yields positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in over 60% of observed cases.
